instructions.title: Portfolio Content Intake
instructions.description: Use this file as the place to paste new content before we convert it into content/*.ts or page copy. Keep it simple. Write in first person. Use bullets. Do not worry about perfect wording on the first pass.
guidelines: Use 'TBD' only when you intentionally do not know something yet.
Do not add claims unless you can back them up.
Write as 'I built,' 'I learned,' 'I decided,' 'I improved.'
Avoid 'Tanmay built,' 'he worked on,' or third-person language.

hero.identity: I build systems that scale, from cloud infrastructure to AI-powered applications.
whatIBuild: Cloud infrastructure and DevOps automation (AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform)
currentFocus: High-signal project building for Summer/Fall 2026 roles

proofPoints.metricOrResult: Delivered 44 production upgrades in 4 months with zero downtime
Reduced deployment downtime by 50% using Blue-Green deployments
Built LSTM model achieving 93.28% accuracy on 400K+ headlines

projects.title: Spendora - Conversational AI for Expense Insights
projects.summary: I built an LLM-powered conversational AI assistant that provides personalized expense insights using retrieval-augmented generation and tool-based reasoning.

problem.whatProblem: How can users get intelligent insights about their spending habits through natural conversation?
problem.whoForIsWas: Anyone wanting AI-powered financial analysis without manual data analysis
problem.whyDidItMatter: Most expense tracking tools lack conversational intelligence and context-aware recommendations

context.constraints: Limited time, focus on LLM integration quality

architecture.backend: LangChain conversational agents with memory management
architecture.dataModelStorage: Custom financial data toolchains, vector embeddings for RAG
architecture.outputUserExperience: Conversational interface with context-aware financial recommendations

technicalDecisions.whyIChoseIt: Simplifies memory management and tool-use patterns for conversational AI
Enables grounding in actual financial data without fine-tuning

tradeoffs: RAG adds latency for retrieval but improves accuracy over hallucination-prone base models
Multiple toolchains provide flexibility but increase complexity

projects.title: News Headline Classification
projects.summary: I built an LSTM-based deep learning model that classifies news headlines with 93.28% accuracy on a dataset of 400K+ headlines.

problem.whatProblem: Automatically categorize news headlines into topics for content organization
problem.whyDidItMatter: Manual classification is time-consuming; automated systems improve discoverability

architecture.backend: LSTM neural network with embedding layers
architecture.dataModelStorage: spaCy for preprocessing, PyTorch for training
architecture.outputUserExperience: Predicted category labels with confidence scores

technicalDecisions.decision: Used LSTM over simpler models like logistic regression
technicalDecisions.whyIChoseIt: Captures sequential dependencies in text better than bag-of-words approaches
technicalDecisions.alternativeConsidered: Transformer-based models, but LSTM was more interpretable

tradeoffs: LSTM training is slower than simpler models but provides better accuracy
Model requires GPU for practical training time

metrics.whatItMeasured: Correct classification rate on test set
Speed of preprocessing and training pipeline

projects.title: Media Recommendation System
projects.summary: I developed a personalized recommendation system for movies, music, and books using multiple algorithms including collaborative filtering, content-based filtering, and clustering.

problem.whatProblem: Help users discover relevant media across different content types
problem.whyDidItMatter: Personalization drives engagement and user satisfaction

architecture.input: User ratings, viewing/listening history, item metadata
architecture.backend: Multiple recommendation algorithms working in ensemble
architecture.dataModelStorage: Flask backend, React frontend, Spotify API integration
architecture.outputUserExperience: Ranked recommendations across movies, music, and books

technicalDecisions.decision: Implemented multiple algorithms (content-based, collaborative, K-means, TF-IDF)
technicalDecisions.whyIChoseIt: Different algorithms capture different recommendation signals
Combines strengths of multiple methods for better recommendations

tradeoffs: Ensemble approach harder to explain than single algorithm

metrics.whatItMeasured: System successfully used content-based, collaborative, K-means, TF-IDF

whatIWouldImproveNext: Implement deep learning models (neural collaborative filtering)

projects.title: SoundScape - Android Music Streaming App
projects.summary: I built an end-to-end Android music streaming app with API integration and intelligent caching that reduced API calls by 70%.

problem.whatProblem: Create a functional music streaming experience on Android
problem.whyDidItMatter: Demonstrates full-stack mobile development and real-world performance optimization

context.constraints: Android platform limitations, API rate limits

architecture.backend: Android SDK with REST API integration
architecture.dataModelStorage: Room database for caching, MediaPlayer for playback
architecture.outputUserExperience: Music streaming player with search, playlist, and playback controls

technicalDecisions.decision: Implemented Room caching layer for Musixmatch API responses
technicalDecisions.whyIChoseIt: Reduces API calls by 70% and improves user experience with instant data
technicalDecisions.alternativeConsidered: No caching, direct API calls (slower, hits rate limits)

technicalDecisions.whyIChoseIt: Separates concerns, makes each component independently testable

tradeoffs: Caching adds storage requirements but dramatically improves performance
Microservices add complexity but improve maintainability

metrics.whatItMeasured: Fewer requests to Musixmatch due to caching

metrics.exactMetric: Full music streaming feature set implemented

whatIWouldImproveNext: Support additional music sources beyond Musixmatch

thinkingPosts.title: How I Evaluate Technical Projects
thinkingPosts.summary: I've developed a framework for evaluating student technical projects based on system design, ML rigor, and real-world viability that I apply as a judge at technical expos.
thinkingPosts.mainPoint: The core idea is that strong technical projects balance three dimensions: architectural soundness, methodological rigor, and practical applicability. Most weak projects fail on one or more of these.

outline.intro: Why I needed a framework when judging at CEAS Expo 2026
outline.point1: System Design - Is the architecture scalable? Do they understand tradeoffs?
outline.point2: Rigor - Did they validate their approach? Are claims backed by evidence?
outline.point3: Real-world Viability - Could this actually work in production? Are edge cases considered?
outline.conclusion: Combining these dimensions reveals project quality beyond surface-level impressive demos

examplesOrStories: Team that built impressive UI but ignored system scalability
Another team with solid architecture but no validation on their ML model claims

thinkingPosts.title: How I'm Running My Job Search Like a System
thinkingPosts.summary: Instead of randomly applying to jobs, I've built a structured system around daily applications, referral outreach, and cold emails to founders/recruiters.
thinkingPosts.mainPoint: The core idea is that job search is a numbers game with a process. By systematizing applications, networking, and follow-ups, I can increase signal and reduce noise in my search for Summer/Fall 2026 roles.

outline.intro: Why random job search doesn't work
outline.point3: Cold email strategy to founders and recruiters

examplesOrStories: How structured daily applications led to interview callbacks
Cold email example that resulted in real opportunity

thinkingPosts.title: What I Look For When Judging Student Projects
thinkingPosts.summary: As a CEAS Expo 2026 judge, I evaluate projects across multiple domains using structured frameworks focused on system design, rigor, and real-world viability.
thinkingPosts.mainPoint: The core idea is that great student projects demonstrate not just implementation ability, but thoughtful decision-making and awareness of constraints and tradeoffs.

outline.point1: How I apply structured evaluation frameworks
outline.point3: What separates good projects from great ones
outline.conclusion: Depth of thinking matters more than feature count

examplesOrStories: Project that impressed with thoughtful architecture decisions
Project that failed despite working demo due to missing rigor

experience.summary: I automated AWS workflows, led infrastructure improvements, and mentored junior engineers while delivering 44 production upgrades in 4 months with zero downtime.
mostImportantImpact: Reduced deployment downtime by 50% using Blue-Green deployments with Ansible
Improved deployment effectiveness by 40% through Python automation and reusable packages
Enhanced code coverage by 200% as Quality Coach and SonarQube Specialist
extraDetails: Mentored 5 junior engineers with documentation and training
Implemented infrastructure-as-code practices improving team velocity
Applied SAFe Practitioner 5.1 principles to DevOps workflows

experience.summary: I developed Go + Terratest framework that caught 95% of infrastructure misconfigurations and automated AWS provisioning, reducing deployment time by 30%.
mostImportantImpact: Automated AWS provisioning with Terraform, reducing deployment time by 30%
Built Go + Terratest testing framework catching 95% of misconfigurations pre-production
Optimized DevOps workflows reducing environment setup from hours to minutes
extraDetails: Enhanced infrastructure validation using Terragrunt for 100% successful Terraform runs
Documented infrastructure-as-code patterns adopted by broader team

experience.summary: I developed a full-stack inward/outward register app that digitized record management and improved department efficiency by 40%.
mostImportantImpact: Developed full-stack React and MySQL application for government record management
Digitized record management improving efficiency by 40%
Deployed system improving transparency and accuracy of government records
extraDetails: Collaborated with government department to understand process requirements
Implemented UI/UX improvements based on user feedback

contact.shortContactNote: I'm actively searching for Summer/Fall 2026 roles in backend engineering, distributed systems, and AI/ML infrastructure. I'm particularly interested in companies building scalable systems or innovative AI products.

resume.notes: Interactive resume with education, skills, experience, and projects

reviewChecklist: I included exact metrics only where I know they are true.
I did not invent dates, links, locations, or results.
I kept each section easy to skim.
I included source notes for important claims.
